conventions.long_only_default: regime decision (Mar-Jun 2026 up-tape); short side untested not falsified

strategies.name: EMA proximity + acceleration, 5m

rules.entry: gap=EMA9-EMA50 (5m); enter long at bar close when |gap|<=0.5, gap<0, dGap>0 (converging up), ddGap>0 (accelerating)
rules.exit: flat at bar close when |gap|<=0.5, gap>0, dGap<0 (anticipated downcross)
rules.stop: none (known gap: no disaster brake; see V0b max loser)
rules.sizing: base 1; +1 when daily 9x50 gap >0 and rising (PF 5.7->8.1, DD unchanged)
rules.disaster_stop: 4*ATR below entry (2*ATR is poison per factorial)
rules.exit_study: NO price trails — all trail schemes destroy P&L (best +1,070 vs signal exit +4,935). Prop overlay: 1/3@2ATR,1/3@4ATR,runner = PF 9.6, DD -244, total halves

caveats: in-sample/tuned window; expect PF~2 live per 15m pseudo-OOS
n=22, top-3 trades = 61% of profit
no Feb-style down-month in dataset
selected from variant grid
next: blind OOS on Dec-Mar 5m batch
test mirrored short config on down months

oos_validation.dataset: DS-1M-DATABENTO resampled to 5m: src/data/MNQ_5m_12mo_databento.csv, 70539 bars, 2025-06-15..2026-06-14, exchange-direct, volume-verified, 0 OHLC violations
oos_validation.config_tested: FROZEN base unchanged: 9x50, k=0.02*ATR(14), accel ON, long-only, NO stop, NO daily-align, friction 1.0pt
oos_validation.method: true blind OOS — vault V4 tuned on TradingView 2026-03-08..06-12; everything before 2026-03-08 is data the model never saw

oos_unseen_all.note: top-3 trades exceed total net profit -> the remaining 40 trades are net-negative

aug2025_feb2026_unseen.note: BREAK-EVEN/DEAD on 7 months of genuinely unseen data

feb2026_correction.note: all 3 trades losers; long-only bleeds in the correction month exactly as caveat warned

is_overlap_new_data.note: re-running the same Mar8-Jun12 window on databento reproduces the vault IS PF (5.66) -> confirms the headline edge was specific to this up-tape window, not the data source

oos_validation.oos_is_pf_ratio: 0.18-0.20 (oos_unseen PF 1.13 / IS PF 5.66 vault or 6.37 new-data) -- WELL BELOW the 0.4 DEGRADED threshold

drift_control.spec: mandatory benchmark: random NY_AM long entries, horizon H=371 5m-bars matched to V4 median hold, n=200 seeded + 200-seed Monte-Carlo
drift_control.vs_v4: V4 full PF 2.38 beats drift 0.83, BUT the outperformance is entirely from the in-sample window; OOS-only PF 1.13 is barely above the drift baseline

oos_validation.verdict: FAILED BLIND OOS / DEGRADED. PF collapses from 5.66 (IS) to ~1.0 on unseen data; OOS/IS ratio 0.18-0.20 << 0.4. The vault headline PF was an artifact of the Mar-Jun 2026 up-tape window, reproducible only on that window. Edge is regime-conditional (up-tape), not a robust standalone long-only edge.
oos_validation.status_recommendation: DEMOTE from FROZEN_AWAITING_OOS via the lifecycle (T11). Do NOT deploy unconditional long-only. Re-frame as regime-gated (deploy only in confirmed up-tape, kill in corrections). Status field left unchanged here pending operator/T11 decision per constitution rule 2.

extreme profit concentration OOS (top-3 = 263% of net)

strategies.name: London zone (OB+FVG) NY tap, multi-touch, long-only

rules.zones: OB: last down-close candle before 1.5xATR displacement breaking 10-bar swing high, formed in London. FVG: 3-bar gaps inside 2xATR/3-bar displacement legs, merged. Zone=full range
rules.survival: multi-touch: zone dies only on 5m CLOSE beyond far edge; <=1 pre-NY touch preferred
rules.entry: zone midpoint on first NY tap
rules.exit: trail under 6-bar swing low after +1R; EOD flatten 15:55 (note: EOD constraint now lifted per operator — retest hold-through variant)

caveats: definitions iterated 4x on same 3-month window — researcher DoF accumulated; expect material OOS haircut

results.note: 1-touch subset PF 7.2 n=10; 2+ touches NEGATIVE PF 0.89

strategies.name: Session H/L interaction day-map

rules.states: London vs Asia: HOLD_HI/HOLD_LO/SWEEP_HI/SWEEP_LO/BOTH/INSIDE; NY first-break side; AM result

results.one_side_days: NY breaks ON low first -> takes ON high same AM only 13% (base 54), n=91; mirror 15% (base 48) n=108
results.london_hold_draw: London holds above Asia -> NY_AM takes ON high 85% (base 54) n=52; mirror 79% n=39
results.am_pm_persistence: AM up -> PM takes AM high 72% (base 47) n=123; AM down -> PM takes AM low 58% (base 35) n=100
results.conflict_cells: London hold + NY breaks opposite side first: 3-4 occurrences in 223 days (structural near-impossibility)
results.reversal_am: ON low first BUT AM closes up -> PM takes AM high 68% n=31 (not Bonferroni-sig; matches sweep-reclaim house pattern)
results.suppressor: non-committed London days (sweep/inside/both): crossover-type systems PF 0.60; suppress trend logic

caveats: liquidity-draw predictions, not close-direction predictions
223 days one regime
next: formalize as context feed for all engines
encode suppressor in risk manager

strategies.name: Shock continuation engine
strategies.dataset: UNBLOCKED 2026-06-15: src/data/MNQ_1m_12mo_databento.csv (352695 1m bars w/ volume, 2025-06-15..2026-06-14, exchange-direct)

rules.spec: docs/SHOCK_ENGINE_SPEC.md: 4-sigma/180s + 3x volume trigger, entry bake-off E1-E4, impulse-scaled stops, trail/duration exits, gamma-regime conditioning

backtest_v1.engine: src/detector/shock.py (a-priori detection) + src/engine/shock_v1.py (productionized bake-off + impulse-scaled stops + trail/duration/scratch exits, sec 4-6); runner research/task_p1_shock.py
backtest_v1.harness_note: src/backtest/harness.py is OHLC-only (no volume) and long-only single-position; it cannot express SHOCK's volume trigger, its DOWN shorts, or the 4-scheme parallel bake-off. The engine is therefore driven by its own event-driven loop over volume-carrying Bars (identical logic a live feed would use). The harness is used only for the mandatory drift control.
backtest_v1.pre_registration: detected at k=4.0 and counted BEFORE any outcome computed: UP=31, DOWN=32. Both <50/direction -> pre-registered one-step fallback to k=3.5 (spec sec 8.1): UP=45, DOWN=53. No further threshold changes.
backtest_v1.detection_threshold_final: k=3.5 (W=180s, m=3.0x vol, A=0.35%, cooldown 30m, sigma EWMA hl=30m, floor 0.25x median|1m logret|)

E2_pullback.note: few fills; misses no-pullback runners by design

E3_extreme_break.note: STANDOUT — edge in continuation past the impulse extreme; BUT top-3 trades = 68% of profit (rule-7 concentration flag)

bakeoff_DOWN.verdict: DOWN-CONTINUATION FALSIFIED. 53 DOWN events (ADEQUATE n, >=50 gate) yet every scheme is PF<1 (E1 0.70, E2 0.07, E3 0.86, E4 0.62) and none beats the 0.77 drift baseline. In the Jun2025-Jun2026 up-tape down-shocks revert -> DOWN is a fade/skip, not continuation (regime-conditional; resolves spec sec 9 open question for this regime).

drift_control.spec: mandatory random-entry benchmark: NY_AM long, H=60min, n=200 + 200-seed MC
drift_control.note: UP E3 PF 3.60 beats the 0.77 random baseline; DOWN schemes sit at/below it

backtest_v1.verdict: SPLIT. (1) UP-shock continuation = UNDER-POWERED: E3 PF 3.60 (n=33, eff_days 28, +850pt) beats drift 0.77, but UP events=45 < the pre-registered 50/direction gate -> NO verdict forced, stays FINDING (and top-3 trades=68% of profit, so even at n>=50 the concentration would bar promotion without more events). (2) DOWN-shock continuation = FALSIFIED (adequate n=53, all schemes PF<1, below drift). Overall SHOCK_V1 stays FINDING: do NOT promote; needs more UP events + gamma feed + slippage model + shadow mode (sec 8.4).
caveats: Small n (UP 45 events, E3 33 fills) — high PF on small n; treat as hypothesis, not validated edge.
gamma_regime UNKNOWN (no dealer-positioning feed) -> the spec's UP-in-SHORT-gamma conditioning was NOT applied; raw all-regime stats.
Fills assume limit/stop execute at the exact intrabar level (only 1pt friction); no gap-through slippage modeled -> optimistic, esp. for E3 stop entries on fast moves.
Trail uses a 15-bar swing-low proxy; time guard simplified to globex 17:00 ET; volume baseline trailing-20d-per-TOD causal, sigma floor full-sample.
Single regime (one up-tape year); DOWN verdict is regime-conditional, not a permanent truth.
blocked on 1-min data with volume -- RESOLVED 2026-06-15 (databento)
UP-shock E3 edge is sub-gate (n<50) and single-regime; not promotable yet
next: gather more UP-shock events (more history / live shadow) to clear the 50-event gate
acquire a gamma-regime feed and re-bucket UP-shocks by SHORT/LONG gamma
model execution slippage for E3 stop entries before trusting PF 3.60
shadow-mode UP-shock E3 >=2 weeks (paper, logged not traded) per spec sec 8.4

results.daymap_gate_rescue: aligned+NY_AM best bucket PF 1.44 n=18 — insufficient

strategies.name: London OB strict-mitigation (superseded)

results.excursion: 15m zones/5m resolution: bull MFE/MAE 1.81 n=66 vs drift control 0.93; bear side dead ~1.0
results.sweep_tap_inversion: tap bar sweeping London extreme -> reversion collapses (MFE/MAE 0.18 n=39, 15m) — sweep+tap = skip/continuation filter

strategies.name: V4 mirror — bearish proximity + acceleration, 5m
strategies.dataset: untested by design — deploy-on-regime; validate on first bear window (Dec-Mar batch and/or live shadow)

rules.entry: SHORT at bar close when |G| <= 0.02*ATR(14), G > 0, dG < 0 (converging down), ddG < 0 (acceleration down)
rules.exit: flat when |G| <= 0.02*ATR(14), G < 0, dG > 0 (anticipated upcross) — gap IS the trail; no price trails
rules.sizing: base 1 unit; +1 unit when DAILY 9x50 gap < 0 AND falling (mirror daily-align)
rules.scale_out_overlay_prop: cover 1/3 at +2*ATR, 1/3 at +4*ATR, runner on signal

caveats: UNTESTED — symmetry assumed, not demonstrated. Known asymmetries: bear approaches faster/more violent (gentle-approach may rarely fire or fire late); squeeze risk; overnight drift against shorts
short side of two-sided prox in Mar-Jun UP-tape was PF 1.00 — expected; says nothing about bear regimes
activation regime-gated by operator (daily gap <0 falling and/or QQQ read)
next: blind test on Feb 2026 window when Dec-Mar data lands

strategies.name: EMA proximity + acceleration, 1m (exploratory timeframe test)
strategies.dataset: DS-1M-EXPLORATORY (not registered): src/data/NQ_1m_aggregated_clean.csv — 2425 1m bars, 2026-06-11 03:35 to 2026-06-12 20:59 UTC (~41h; tail of DS-5M window, NOT independent OOS); cleaned 0 dupes / 0 OHLC violations / 0 overlap conflicts

rules.config: base V4 made comparable to the 5m vault base: k=0.02*ATR(14), acceleration ON (ddG>0), long-only, NO daily-align, NO stop; friction 1.0pt; EMAs 9/50 ewm adjust=False; ATR simple rolling-mean

drift_control.spec: mandatory benchmark: random same-session NY_AM long entries, horizon H=41 1m-bars (matched to V4's single 41-min hold), n=200 seeded + 200-seed Monte-Carlo

results.vs_benchmark: V4 PF 0.00 < drift PF 1.59; drift MFE/MAE ~1.0 (neutral up-tape window). V4 does NOT beat the benchmark — but n=1 so statistically meaningless

caveats: EXPLORATORY / UNTESTED new timeframe — do NOT promote. Logged because negative results are first-class (prevents re-testing a dead config blindly).

Signal essentially does not fire on 1m: only 1 entry in 2425 bars. k=0.02*ATR on 1m yields a tiny proximity band; the engine likely needs timeframe-specific k retuning before any 1m conclusion is valid.
Data window ~2 days (Jun 11-12 2026) is the TAIL of the 5m vault window — not independent OOS and far too short (the 5m base ran 3 months / 19168 bars for its +4935).
n=1, a single -13.8pt loser — no statistical inference; not comparable to the 5m vault headline.
1m export carries no volume; a full multi-month 1m series (ideally with volume) is required before evaluating this timeframe.
Source is full-size NQ (~$20/pt); 5m/15m vault data is the MNQ export — point magnitudes comparable, $ values differ.
next: acquire a full multi-month, independent-window 1m NQ export before any further 1m test
if pursued, retune k to the 1m ATR scale and re-run with the drift control

strategies.name: V4 on native 1-minute timeframe, 12mo databento (exploratory)

rules.config: frozen V4 base on 1m closes: 9x50, accel ON, long-only, NO stop, NO daily-align, friction 1.0pt
rules.k_derivation: k=0.02*ATR(14) retained — already volatility-relative so it transfers without fitting (1m ATR median 7.48 -> 0.15pt band vs 5m 17.66 -> 0.35pt). Sensitivity scan k_mult 0.02->0.20 shows PF degrading monotonically (0.02->1.29, 0.04->0.88, 0.06->0.78, 0.10->0.71); matching the 5m ABSOLUTE band (k_mult~0.047) is already PF<1. No k makes 1m profitable — selection by structure/frequency, not P&L.

oos_unseen_pre_mar2026.note: barely above breakeven; the other 231 trades are net-negative

drift_control.spec: mandatory benchmark: random NY_AM long entries, H=294 1m-bars (V4 median hold), n=200 seeded + 200-seed MC

results.vs_benchmark: 1m full PF 1.29 marginally beats drift 0.90; OOS-only PF 1.07 is at the drift baseline

caveats: EXPLORATORY new timeframe — do NOT promote.
Edge does NOT survive at 1m: full PF 1.29, blind-OOS PF 1.07 (vs 5m in-sample 5.66, 5m blind-OOS ~1.0). Higher frequency adds noise; even the in-sample window only reaches PF 1.74.
Supersedes the earlier 2-day stub EMA_PROX_V4_1M_TEST (n=1 on a 2-day volumeless TradingView file); this is the proper 12-month, volume-verified, exchange-direct test.
Extreme profit concentration OOS (top-3 trades = 209% of net profit).
1m holds average ~9.6h — still multi-hour swing behavior, not scalping; the timeframe change did not convert V4 into a high-frequency edge.
next: do not pursue 1m for V4 as a standalone edge; 5m remains the V4 timeframe
retain 1m data for SHOCK_v1 and microstructure work

strategies.name: NY-open bullish order-block reversion, 1m (single-entry)
strategies.dataset: src/data/MNQ_1m_12mo_databento.csv (352695 1m bars w/ volume, 2025-06-15..2026-06-14, exchange-direct Databento, front-month stitched U5/Z5/H6/M6)
strategies.finding: Single-entry NY-open bull OB has NO edge as specified. In-repo on 12mo databento it is a net LOSER (n=164, PF 0.94, -150.5pt, 7/13 green). The external winning number (n=798, PF 2.26, +$15,968) was a RE-ENTRY artifact of the external pipeline (multiple re-entries per zone), NOT reproducible under one-entry-per-zone. The re-entry variant is spec'd separately as OB_NYOPEN_REENTRY_1M (SPEC_ONLY) for honest testing.
strategies.provenance: Verified in-repo (T24, 2026-06-15) via src/engine/ob_nyopen.py through the harness; operator confirmed the external 798/2.26 was a multiple-re-entry artifact the single-entry vault spec did not capture. Engine NOT tuned. In-repo numbers below are the verified record.

rules.setup: bullish order-block reversion at the NY open -- ONE entry per zone (first same-day tap only)
rules.displacement: single bar, body >= 1.5*ATR(14), breaks the prior 10-bar high
rules.entry: long at the zone top edge on the first same-day tap
rules.exit: stop 1pt below zone low; target = entry + 2*max(zone_height,3); 120-min max hold

results.verdict: net LOSER -- no edge as specified
results.pnl_units: points, friction 1pt RT (engine run on MNQ 1m data)

external_artifact_rejected.note: re-entry artifact; NOT reproducible under single-entry; do NOT use -- see OB_NYOPEN_REENTRY_1M

in_repo_verification.spec_implemented: literal per T24: OB = last down candle before a >=1.5*ATR(14) up-displacement candle (body) that breaks the prior 10-bar high, formed 09:30-11:00 ET; enter long at first same-day tap (zone top edge); stop 1pt below zone low; target = entry + 2*max(zone_height,3); 120-min max hold; friction 1pt; single position at a time (harness constraint)
in_repo_verification.result: MISMATCH -- could NOT reproduce the operator-external numbers
in_repo_verification.diagnosis: Qualitative mismatch, not a near-miss (in-repo loses money; external strongly wins). Entry count is ~5x too low and the gap CANNOT be tuned away: OB FORMATIONS in the 09:30-11:00 window number only 210 (body-displacement) or 551 (range-displacement) over 12 months -- both fewer than the 798 ENTRIES claimed. 798 entries cannot arise from a single-displacement-bar, one-entry-per-zone, 09:30-11:00 reading. The external pipeline must use a different setup definition (e.g. multi-bar displacement leg, multiple taps / re-entries per zone, concurrent positions, wider window, different zone edge or displacement metric) not captured by the vault description.
in_repo_verification.spec_inconsistency: This entry's rules.window says '09:30-09:50 ET' but the T24 task spec said '09:30-11:00 ET' -- the formation window is itself ambiguous across sources; implemented per the T24 instruction.
in_repo_verification.no_tuning_attestation: src/engine/ob_nyopen.py is a faithful literal implementation of the description; NO parameters were adjusted toward the target numbers (operator directive).
in_repo_verification.action_needed: Operator to supply the exact OB setup definition (displacement metric, window, tap/re-entry and position-concurrency rules) so the engine can be reconciled. Status left CANDIDATE but numbers are UNCONFIRMED; do not promote.

caveats: Single-entry version FALSIFIED on 12mo databento (T24); do not deploy and do not re-propose without a structural change.
Negative result is first-class -- it prevents rediscovering this dead config; the external 798/2.26 was a re-entry artifact, not an edge.
This falsification is of the SINGLE-ENTRY rule only; it says nothing about the separate re-entry hypothesis OB_NYOPEN_REENTRY_1M.
next: test the re-entry variant (OB_NYOPEN_REENTRY_1M) honestly per its spec
do not re-propose single-entry NY-open OB without a structural change

strategies.name: NY-open bullish OB reversion WITH re-entry, 1m
strategies.provenance: Spec'd then BACKTESTED in-repo 2026-06-15 (src/engine/ob_reentry.py via the harness; runner research/task_p2_ob_reentry.py). Result is the verified record. Engine NOT tuned.
strategies.finding: Re-entry does NOT rescue the NY-open OB reversion -- it makes it WORSE. Net LOSER PF 0.81, -1008.5pt (vs single-entry -150.5pt): each re-tap of a valid zone just adds another correlated losing trade. Does not clear PF>1.5 and is below breakeven. Also does NOT reproduce the external n=798/PF2.26 (faithful re-entry gives n=350, PF 0.81) -- so that external number remains unexplained by this definition (likely concurrency + other undocumented differences), but regardless there is no edge to capture.

rules.displacement: single bar, body >= 1.5*ATR(14), breaks the prior 10-bar high
rules.zone: the last down candle before the displacement; zone = [low, high]
rules.zone_validity: zone stays VALID until a 1m CLOSE beyond its far edge (a 1m close below zone low invalidates it); intrabar taps/wicks do NOT invalidate
rules.re_entry: RE-ENTRY ALLOWED -- the key difference vs FALSIFIED OB_NYOPEN_BULL_1M: each time price exits the zone and later re-taps a STILL-VALID zone, that is a NEW long entry (a zone can produce multiple entries until invalidated)
rules.entry: long at the zone top edge on each (re)tap
rules.exit: stop 1pt below zone low; target = entry + 2*max(zone_height,3); 120-min max hold per entry
rules.concurrency: SINGLE position at a time (no concurrency). The current harness is single-position: if a re-tap fires while a position is open, SKIP it (do not queue) and log the count of skipped overlapping taps. Revisit if/when a multi-position harness exists.

overcounting.note: re-entry ~doubled n vs single-entry (164->350) and made P&L worse (-150->-1008); re-entries are correlated losers

drift_control.spec: mandatory: random NY_AM long H=60, n=200 + 200-seed MC
drift_control.note: PF 0.81 > drift 0.77 numerically, but BOTH are <1.0 (losing) -- beating a sub-1 baseline is not an edge

caveats: FALSIFIED on 12mo databento (net loser PF 0.81); do not deploy. Re-entry is the documented difference vs the single-entry version and it did not help.
Overcounting confirmed: 46% of the 350 trades are re-entries on 188 zones (up to 7/zone); they are correlated and net-negative -- exactly the red flag to watch.
Single-position harness skipped 645 overlapping taps; a concurrent book would take more of the same negative-expectancy trades, not fewer -- concurrency would not turn this positive.
Does not reproduce the external n=798/PF2.26; that number is not explained by faithful re-entry and is not pursued further (no edge exists).
next: do not pursue NY-open OB reversion (single-entry and re-entry both falsified on 12mo data)

Consistent with the prior vault lesson that indicator/volume-confirm filters have repeatedly failed to add edge in this market.

caveats: Use the 09:30-09:50 open window in OB_NYOPEN_BULL_1M; the 09:50 macro window is falsified as a timing filter.

strategies.name: V4 proximity + acceleration on 15m (frozen base), 12mo databento
strategies.provenance: In-repo verification (priority-3, 2026-06-15) of the operator-external 'V4_long_15m OOS PF 3.67 / DD -$1,080' claim. src/engine/v4.py through the harness; runner research/task_p3_v4_15m.py. Engine NOT tuned.

rules.config: frozen base: 9x50, k=0.02*ATR(14), accel ON, long-only, NO stop, NO daily-align, friction 1.0pt (same config as the 5m frozen V4, applied to 15m bars)
rules.note: k=0.02*ATR base matches the operator number; k=0.75 (PF 3.75 but DD -$1,959) and k=1.5 (PF 2.53) do NOT fit the -$1,080 DD

verification.reconciliation: RECONCILED within tolerance: in-repo PF 3.57 vs claimed 3.67 (delta 0.10); maxDD -$989 vs -$1,080 (delta ~$90). Small deltas attributable to resample/convention. Operator claim CONFIRMED.

in_repo_blind_oos_pre_mar2026.note: the genuinely-unseen sub-window is only PF 1.91 -- the headline 3.57 is dominated by the in-sample Mar-Jun up-tape window, like the 5m case

drift_control.spec: random NY_AM long H=120 15m-bars, n=200 + 200-seed MC

results.prop_fit: maxDD -$989 fits the $2K trailing prop limit (unlike V4 5m -$2,582)

caveats: VERY LOW n (19 trades, eff_days 19) and EXTREME concentration: top-3 trades = 92% of profit (constitution rule-7 red flag). PF 3.57 is not a robust edge.
Headline is up-tape-window-driven: the blind pre-Mar-2026 sub-window is only PF 1.91. Same regime-conditional pattern as V4 5m.
Beats drift (3.57 vs 1.33) and clears PF>1.5, and DD fits the prop limit -> kept CANDIDATE, but n is well below 'adequate'; needs more out-of-sample months before any promotion.
Which exact k the operator used is inferred (base k=0.02*ATR matches best on both PF and DD); not independently confirmed.
next: walk-forward OOS across more windows before trusting PF 3.57 (n far too small)
